title: Oracle JDBC live-validation: resume Docker read-only DB run
description:
PAUSED/BLOCKED (external): Part B live-validation tooling is authored+typechecks but the real-DB run is blocked by a Docker Desktop 4.82.0 crash-loop; fix = Windows reboot. Exact resume steps: docs/ai/ORACLE_LIVE_VALIDATION_RESUME.md. Branch feature/oracle-jdbc-driver-settings.

## Context
Blocker: orphaned AF_UNIX Model-Runner socket; reboot clears it. Model Runner already disabled in Docker settings-store.json.
notes: Pivot: user provided a LOCAL Oracle 19c (jdbc:oracle:thin:@//localhost:1521/ORCLPDB, user SPECTER_READER, schema SPECTER_FIXTURE) instead of the Docker FREEPDB1/SPECTER_RO path. Docker not needed. Running the app-level verify:oracle-live gate against this DB via the Settings-managed driver bundle.
close_reason: verify:oracle-live PASSED 7/7 in real mode against the local Oracle 19c (ORCLPDB / least-privilege SPECTER_READER) via Settings bundle ojdbc17 (JDBC-only). Docker not needed. Fixture: scripts/oracle/local-19c-awkit-types-fixture.sql (SPECTER_FIXTURE.AWKIT_TYPES_TEST, 204 rows, SYS-created synonym). Ephemeral credential minted, used, rotated+locked. Part B still uncommitted.

title: Revoke other sessions on password change (session rotation)
description: Finding from awkit-ekd review: AuthenticationService.changePassword does not revoke the user's other active sessions. Add revoke-all-except-current after a successful change. Add a verify:auth case.

title: SecurityStore: cross-process single-writer lock (DurableLockStore) + single-instance app lock
description: Finding from awkit-ekd review: SecurityStore has no cross-process mutex and app sets no requestSingleInstanceLock; two AWKIT processes can lose writes to security.sqlite. Reuse src/runner/store/DurableLockStore around SecurityStore writes (as SqliteRuntimeStore does) and/or add app.requestSingleInstanceLock in app/main/main.ts. Plan §17.

title: branding-splash broke firstWindow()-based Electron GUI verifiers
description: The branding-splash feature (PR #12) shows a splash BrowserWindow first, so Playwright's app.firstWindow() now returns the splash — which has no window.playwrightFlowStudio preload bridge. Every GUI verifier that does app.firstWindow() then win.evaluate(() => window.playwrightFlowStudio...) throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading settings)'. Confirmed on verify:settings-persistence (fixed in the oracle epic via a resolveMainWindow helper that polls app.windows() for the bridge). Likely also affects verify:capacity-settings-gui, verify:instance-monitor-gui, verify:runtime-analytics-gui, verify:workflow-builder, verify:flow-designer, verify:reports (all use _electron + firstWindow). Fix: add a shared resolveMainWindow(app) helper (see scripts/verify-oracle-drivers-gui.mjs / verify-settings-persistence.mjs) that waits for the window exposing window.playwrightFlowStudio.settings, and use it instead of firstWindow(). NOT an Oracle-refactor defect.
